steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is C, $x(19x^2 + 3x + 8)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Identify the greatest common factor
steps.content: Every term in $19x^3 + 3x^2 + 8x$ contains at least one factor of $x$. The GCF is $x$.

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The result $x(19x^2 + 3x + 8)$ matches the correct answer exactly.

explanation:
• Choice A — $x(x^2 + 3x + 8)$: Expanding gives $x^3 + 3x^2 + 8x$, missing the coefficient 19 on the cubic term. ✗
  • Choice B — $x(19x^3 + 3x^2 + 1)$: Expanding gives $19x^4 + 3x^3 + x$, which changes the degree and loses the 8x term. ✗
  • Choice C — $x(19x^2 + 3x + 8)$: Expanding gives $19x^3 + 3x^2 + 8x$, which is exactly the original expression. ✓
  • Choice D — $30\left(x^3 + x^2 + x\right)$: Expanding gives $30x^3 + 30x^2 + 30x$; the coefficients 19, 3, 8 are gone and replaced by 30. ✗
C is correct.
